steps.title: Factor the radicand
steps.content: $x^2 + 8x + 16$ is a perfect square trinomial because $8 = 2 \cdot 4$ and $16 = 4^2$. So $x^2 + 8x + 16 = (x + 4)^2$, and since $x > 0$, $x + 4 > 0$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• $(x+4)^4$ — this would require raising the radicand to the 16th power, not taking a fourth root. Way too big.
  • $(x+4)^2$ — equals the radicand itself, not its fourth root.
  • $(x+4)$ — equals the square root of the radicand, not the fourth root.
  • $(x+4)^{1/2}$ — matches the exponent $2/4 = 1/2$ from the calculation above.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
